stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" focusable=\"false\" class=\"size-3 transition-transform text-gray-400 group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 shrink-0 rotate-90\"><path d=\"M6.5 2.75L12.75 9L6.5 15.25\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></button></div></header><div class=\"mdx-content @container/columns-container relative mt-8 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [contain:inline-size] isolate empty:hidden mb-14\" data-page-title=\"Configure the sandboxed Bash tool\" data-page-href=\"/en/sandboxing\" id=\"content\"><span data-as=\"p\">The Bash sandbox lets Claude run most shell commands without stopping to ask permission. Instead of approving each command, you define which files and network domains commands can touch, and the operating system enforces that boundary for every Bash command and its child processes.</span>\n<div role=\"note\" aria-label=\"Note\" class=\"callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-600/20\" data-callout-type=\"note\"><div class=\"mt-0.5 w-4\" data-component-part=\"callout-icon\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-4 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300\"><path d=\"M 9 1.6714 C 13.0371 1.6714 16.3286 4.9629 16.3286 9 C 16.3286 13.0371 13.0371 16.3286 9 16.3286 C 7.0574 16.3252 5.1953 15.552 3.8217 14.1783 C 2.448 12.8047 1.6748 10.9426 1.6714 9 C 1.6714 4.9629 4.9629 1.6714 9 1.6714 Z M 9 0 C 4.0371 0 0 4.0371 0 9 C 0 13.9629 4.0371 18 9 18 C 13.9629 18 18 13.9629 18 9 C 18 4.0371 13.9629 0 9 0 Z M 10.2857 3.8571 H 7.7143 V 10.2857 H 10.2857 V 3.8571 Z M 10.2857 11.5714 H 7.7143 V 14.1429 H 10.2857 V 11.5714 Z\" fill=\"currentColor\" stroke=\"none\" fill-rule=\"evenodd\" clip-rule=\"evenodd\"></path></svg></div><div class=\"text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&amp;_code]:text-current! [&amp;_kbd]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:border-current [&amp;_strong]:text-current! text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300\" data-component-part=\"callout-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">To compare other isolation approaches such as dev containers, custom containers, and virtual machines, see <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/sandbox-environments\">Sandbox environments</a>. To reduce permission prompts for tools other than Bash, see <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes\">permission modes</a>.</span></div></div>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"get-started\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#get-started\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Get started</span></h2>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The sandbox is built into Claude Code and runs on macOS, Linux, and WSL2. Native Windows is not supported. On Windows, run Claude Code inside a WSL2 distribution.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">On macOS, there is nothing to install: sandboxing uses the built-in Seatbelt framework. On Linux and WSL2, the sandbox relies on two packages, covered in <a href=\"#set-up-linux-and-wsl2\" class=\"link\">Set up Linux and WSL2</a>. Even if you haven’t installed them yet, you can start with <code>/sandbox</code>, because its panel shows whether anything is missing.</span>\n<div role=\"list\" class=\"steps ml-3.5 mt-10 mb-6\"><div role=\"listitem\" class=\"step group/step step-container relative flex items-start pb-5\"><div data-component-part=\"step-line\" class=\"absolute w-px h-[calc(100%-2.75rem)] top-11 bg-gray-200/70 dark:bg-white/10\" contenteditable=\"false\"></div><div class=\"absolute ml-[-13px] py-2\" data-component-part=\"step-number\" contenteditable=\"false\"><div class=\"group/step-indicator relative size-7 shrink-0 rounded-full bg-gray-50 dark:bg-white/10 text-xs text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-50 font-semibold flex items-center justify-center\"><div class=\"group-has-[a:focus-visible]/step-indicator:opacity-0\">1</div></div></div><div class=\"w-full overflow-hidden pl-8 pr-px\"><p class=\"mt-2 font-semibold prose dark:prose-invert text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-200\" contenteditable=\"false\" data-component-part=\"step-title\">Run /sandbox</p><div data-component-part=\"step-content\" class=\"prose dark:prose-invert\"><span data-as=\"p\">Start a Claude Code session and run the <code>/sandbox</code> command:</span><div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 **:outline-0 **:focus:ring-0 **:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white\"><div role=\"presentation\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_cld1dbf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] py-3.5 px-4 overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"min-w-full! h-full children:my-0! children:shadow-none! children:bg-transparent!\"><div class=\"font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6\"><pre class=\"shiki shiki-themes github-light-default dark-plus\" style=\"background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E;color:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\" language=\"text\"><code language=\"text\" numberoflines=\"2\"><span class=\"line\"><span>/sandbox</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span></span></span></code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div><span data-as=\"p\">This opens the sandbox panel with three tabs, plus a Dependencies tab on Linux when the optional seccomp filter is missing:</span><ul>\n<li><strong>Mode</strong>: choose how sandboxed commands are approved, covered in the next step</li>\n<li><strong>Overrides</strong>: choose whether commands that fail under the sandbox can fall back to running unsandboxed. This is the <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>allowUnsandboxedCommands</code></a> setting</li>\n<li><strong>Config</strong>: view the resolved sandbox settings</li>\n</ul><span data-as=\"p\">If the panel shows only a Dependencies tab, a required package is missing. Install it as described in <a href=\"#set-up-linux-and-wsl2\" class=\"link\">Set up Linux and WSL2</a>, restart Claude Code, and run <code>/sandbox</code> again.</span></div></div></div><div role=\"listitem\" class=\"step group/step step-container relative flex items-start pb-5\"><div data-component-part=\"step-line\" class=\"absolute w-px h-[calc(100%-2.75rem)] top-11 bg-gray-200/70 dark:bg-white/10\" contenteditable=\"false\"></div><div class=\"absolute ml-[-13px] py-2\" data-component-part=\"step-number\" contenteditable=\"false\"><div class=\"group/step-indicator relative size-7 shrink-0 rounded-full bg-gray-50 dark:bg-white/10 text-xs text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-50 font-semibold flex items-center justify-center\"><div class=\"group-has-[a:focus-visible]/step-indicator:opacity-0\">2</div></div></div><div class=\"w-full overflow-hidden pl-8 pr-px\"><p class=\"mt-2 font-semibold prose dark:prose-invert text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-200\" contenteditable=\"false\" data-component-part=\"step-title\">Choose a mode</p><div data-component-part=\"step-content\" class=\"prose dark:prose-invert\"><span data-as=\"p\">On the Mode tab, select auto-allow or regular permissions. Auto-allow runs sandboxed commands without prompting, and regular permissions keeps the regular permission prompts even when commands are sandboxed. See <a href=\"#sandbox-modes\" class=\"link\">Sandbox modes</a> for which commands still prompt in auto-allow mode.</span></div></div></div><div role=\"listitem\" class=\"step group/step step-container relative flex items-start pb-5\"><div data-component-part=\"step-line\" class=\"absolute w-px h-[calc(100%-2.75rem)] top-11 bg-transparent bg-linear-to-b from-gray-200 dark:from-white/10 via-80% to-transparent group-has-[[data-component-part=&quot;step-content&quot;]:empty]/step:hidden\" contenteditable=\"false\"></div><div class=\"absolute ml-[-13px] py-2\" data-component-part=\"step-number\" contenteditable=\"false\"><div class=\"group/step-indicator relative size-7 shrink-0 rounded-full bg-gray-50 dark:bg-white/10 text-xs text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-50 font-semibold flex items-center justify-center\"><div class=\"group-has-[a:focus-visible]/step-indicator:opacity-0\">3</div></div></div><div class=\"w-full overflow-hidden pl-8 pr-px\"><p class=\"mt-2 font-semibold prose dark:prose-invert text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-200\" contenteditable=\"false\" data-component-part=\"step-title\">Run a Bash command</p><div data-component-part=\"step-content\" class=\"prose dark:prose-invert\"><span data-as=\"p\">Ask Claude to run a command, such as a build or a test suite. By default, commands inside the sandbox can write only to the working directory and the session temp directory. The first time a command needs a new network domain, Claude Code prompts for approval.</span><span data-as=\"p\">Commands that cannot run sandboxed fall back to the regular permission flow. To widen or narrow these boundaries, see <a href=\"#configure-sandboxing\" class=\"link\">Configure sandboxing</a>.</span></div></div></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Selecting a mode in the panel writes to your project’s local settings at <code>.claude/settings.local.json</code>, which apply to the current project and are not checked into git. To enable the sandbox across all of your projects, set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>sandbox.enabled</code></a> to <code>true</code> in your user settings at <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code>. To enforce sandboxing for every developer in an organization, use <a href=\"#enforce-sandboxing-with-managed-settings\" class=\"link\">managed settings</a>.</span>\n<div role=\"note\" aria-label=\"Warning\" class=\"callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-yellow-200 bg-yellow-50 dark:border-yellow-900 dark:bg-yellow-600/20 [&amp;_[data-component-part='callout-icon']]:mt-px\" data-callout-type=\"warning\"><div class=\"mt-0.5 w-4\" data-component-part=\"callout-icon\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"flex-none size-5 text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-300\"><path d=\"M 9 6.75 v 1.5 m 0 3 h 0.0075 m -5.2035 3 h 10.392 c 1.155 0 1.8765 -1.2503 1.299 -2.25 L 10.299 3 c -0.5775 -0.9997 -2.0205 -0.9997 -2.598 0 L 2.505 12 c -0.5775 0.9997 0.144 2.25 1.299 2.25 z\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div><div class=\"text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&amp;_code]:text-current! [&amp;_kbd]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:border-current [&amp;_strong]:text-current! text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-300\" data-component-part=\"callout-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">By default, if the sandbox cannot start because dependencies are missing or the platform is unsupported, Claude Code shows a warning and runs commands without sandboxing. To make this a hard failure instead, set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>sandbox.failIfUnavailable</code></a> to <code>true</code>. This is intended for managed deployments that require sandboxing as a security gate.</span></div></div>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"set-up-linux-and-wsl2\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#set-up-linux-and-wsl2\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Set up Linux and WSL2</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">On Linux and WSL2, the sandbox relies on two packages:</span>\n<ul>\n<li><a href=\"https://github.com/containers/bubblewrap\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer\" class=\"link\"><code>bubblewrap</code></a>: the unprivileged sandboxing tool that enforces filesystem isolation</li>\n<li><a href=\"http://www.dest-unreach.org/socat/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer\" class=\"link\"><code>socat</code></a>: the relay used to route network traffic through the sandbox proxy</li>\n</ul>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Install them with your distribution’s package manager:</span>\n<div class=\"tabs tabs tab-container\" id=\"ubuntu%2Fdebian\"><ul role=\"tablist\" class=\"not-prose mb-6 pb-[1px] flex-none min-w-full overflow-auto border-b border-gray-200 gap-x-6 flex dark:border-gray-200/10\" data-component-part=\"tabs-list\"><li id=\"ubuntu%2Fdebian\" role=\"tab\" aria-selected=\"true\" aria-controls=\"panel-ubuntu%2Fdebian-0\" tabindex=\"0\" class=\"cursor-pointer\"><div class=\"flex text-sm items-center gap-1.5 leading-6 data-testid=\"tab-Ubuntu/Debian\"> <!-- -->Ubuntu/Debian</div></li><li id=\"fedora\" role=\"tab\" aria-selected=\"false\" aria-controls=\"panel-fedora-1\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"cursor-pointer\"><div class=\"flex text-sm items-center gap-1.5 leading-6 font-semibold whitespace-nowrap pt-3 pb-2.5 -mb-px max-w-max border-b text-gray-900 border-transparent hover:border-gray-300 dark:text-gray-200 dark:hover:border-gray-700\" data-component-part=\"tab-button\" data-active=\"false\" data-testid=\"tab-Fedora\"> <!-- -->Fedora</div></li></ul><div><div id=\"panel-ubuntu%2Fdebian-0\" [&amp;&gt;:first-child:not(p)]:mt-0 [&amp;&gt;:first-child:not(p)_img]:mt-0 [&amp;&gt;:first-child[data-table-wrapper]]:pt-0!\" data-component-part=\"tab-content\"><div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 dark:bg-transparent\" numberoflines=\"1\" language=\"shellscript\"><div data-floating-buttons=\"true\" class=\"absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden\"><div class=\"z-10 language=\"shellscript\"><code language=\"shellscript\" numberoflines=\"1\"><span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#953800;--shiki-dark:#DCDCAA\">sudo</span><span style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div></div></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">When a dependency is missing, the Dependencies tab in <code>/sandbox</code> lists which of <code>ripgrep</code>, <code>bubblewrap</code>, <code>socat</code>, and the seccomp filter your platform lacks. If you don’t see the tab after installing and restarting Claude Code, all dependencies are present.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Ripgrep is bundled with the native Claude Code binary. The seccomp filter is optional and adds Unix domain socket blocking. Install it with <code>npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime</code> if it is missing.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">When a required dependency is missing, the Dependencies tab is the only tab shown until you install it. When only the optional seccomp filter is missing, the Dependencies tab appears alongside the other tabs. The dependency check runs at startup, so restart Claude Code after installing packages for <code>/sandbox</code> to detect them.</span>\n<div class=\"accordion-group [&amp;&gt;details]:border-0 and later: allow bubblewrap to create user namespaces</p></div></summary><div id=\"ubuntu-24-04-and-later-allow-bubblewrap-to-create-user-namespaces-accordion-children\" role=\"region\" aria-labelledby=\"ubuntu-24-04-and-later-allow-bubblewrap-to-create-user-namespaces-accordion-title\" class=\"mt-2 mb-4 mx-6 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert overflow-x-auto cursor-default\" data-component-part=\"accordion-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">On Ubuntu 24.04 and later, the default AppArmor policy prevents bubblewrap from creating the user namespaces it needs for isolation.</span><span data-as=\"p\">To check whether your environment enforces this restriction, including inside WSL2, run <code>sysctl kernel.apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns</code>. If the command returns <code>0</code>, skip this step. If it prints a <code>No such file or directory</code> error, the key doesn’t exist and you can skip this step. If it returns <code>1</code>, add an AppArmor profile that grants <code>bwrap</code> this capability:</span><div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 language=\"shellscript\"><code language=\"shellscript\" numberoflines=\"1\"><span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#953800;--shiki-dark:#DCDCAA\">sudo</span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\"> systemctl</span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\"> reload</span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\"> apparmor</span></span>\n</code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div></div></details><details class=\"accordion border-standard rounded-2xl mb-3 overflow-hidden bg-background-light dark:bg-codeblock cursor-default\"><summary class=\"relative not-prose flex flex-row items-center content-center w-full cursor-pointer list-none [&amp;::-webkit-details-marker]:hidden py-4 px-5 [&amp;&gt;div]:ml-2 [&amp;&gt;div]:mr-0 hover:bg-gray-100 hover:dark:bg-gray-800 rounded-t-xl\" aria-controls=\"wsl2-notes-accordion-children\" aria-expanded=\"false\" data-component-part=\"accordion-button\"><div id=\"wsl2-notes\" class=\"absolute top-[-8rem]\"></div><div class=\"mr-0.5\" data-component-part=\"accordion-caret-right\"><svg class=\"h-3 w-3 transition bg-gray-700 dark:bg-gray-400 duration-75\" aria-hidden=\"true\" focusable=\"false\" style=\"-webkit-mask-image:url(&quot;https://d3gk2c5xim1je2.cloudfront.net/fontawesome/v7.2.0/solid/caret-right.svg&quot;);-webkit-mask-repeat:no-repeat;-webkit-mask-position:center;mask-image:url(&quot;https://d3gk2c5xim1je2.cloudfront.net/fontawesome/v7.2.0/solid/caret-right.svg&quot;);mask-repeat:no-repeat;mask-position:center\"></svg></div><div class=\"leading-tight text-left w-full\" contenteditable=\"false\" data-component-part=\"accordion-title-container\"><p id=\"wsl2-notes-accordion-title\" class=\"m-0 font-medium text-gray-900 dark:text-gray-200\" data-component-part=\"accordion-title\">WSL2 notes</p></div></summary><div id=\"wsl2-notes-accordion-children\" role=\"region\" aria-labelledby=\"wsl2-notes-accordion-title\" class=\"mt-2 mb-4 mx-6 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert overflow-x-auto cursor-default\" data-component-part=\"accordion-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">Check your WSL version with <code>wsl -l -v</code> from PowerShell. If you see <code>Sandboxing requires WSL2</code>, your distribution is running WSL1. Upgrade it to WSL2 or run Claude Code without sandboxing.</span><span data-as=\"p\">On WSL2, sandboxed commands cannot launch Windows binaries such as <code>cmd.exe</code>, <code>powershell.exe</code>, or anything under <code>/mnt/c/</code>. WSL hands these off to the Windows host over a Unix socket, which the sandbox blocks. If a command needs to invoke a Windows binary, add it to <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>excludedCommands</code></a> so it runs outside the sandbox.</span></div></details></div>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"sandbox-modes\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#sandbox-modes\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Sandbox modes</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Claude Code offers two sandbox modes:</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\"><strong>Auto-allow mode</strong>: Bash commands will attempt to run inside the sandbox and are automatically allowed without requiring permission. Commands that cannot be sandboxed, such as those needing network access to non-allowed hosts, fall back to the regular permission flow, where Claude Code checks your <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions\">permission rules</a> and gates any command those rules do not already allow, with a prompt in default mode or the classifier in <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes#eliminate-prompts-with-auto-mode\">auto mode</a>.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Even in auto-allow mode, the following still apply:</span>\n<ul>\n<li>Explicit <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions\">deny rules</a> are always respected</li>\n<li><code>rm</code> or <code>rmdir</code> commands that target <code>/</code>, your home directory, or other critical system paths still trigger a permission prompt</li>\n<li>Content-scoped <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions\">ask rules</a> like <code>Bash(git push *)</code> still force a prompt even for sandboxed commands</li>\n<li>A bare <code>Bash</code> ask rule, or the equivalent <code>Bash(*)</code> form, is skipped for commands that run sandboxed; it still applies to commands that fall back to the regular permission flow</li>\n</ul>\n<span data-as=\"p\"><strong>Regular permissions mode</strong>: All Bash commands go through the regular permission flow, even when sandboxed. This provides more control but requires more approvals.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">In both modes, the sandbox enforces the same filesystem and network restrictions. The difference is only in whether sandboxed commands are auto-approved or require explicit permission.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The session temp directory is writable inside the sandbox by default, alongside the working directory. Claude Code sets <code>$TMPDIR</code> to this directory for sandboxed commands, so tools that write temporary files work without extra configuration. Unsandboxed commands inherit your shell’s <code>$TMPDIR</code> unchanged, which means sandboxed and unsandboxed commands resolve <code>$TMPDIR</code> to different directories. To pass temporary files between the two, write them under the working directory instead.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Some commands cannot run inside the sandbox at all, such as tools that are incompatible with it or that need a host you have not allowed. Rather than failing the task or requiring you to turn sandboxing off, Claude Code includes an escape hatch: when a command fails because of sandbox restrictions, Claude analyzes the failure and may retry the command with the <code>dangerouslyDisableSandbox</code> parameter. The retried command runs outside the sandbox, so it goes through the regular permission flow: in default mode you get a confirmation prompt; in <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes#eliminate-prompts-with-auto-mode\">auto mode</a> the classifier evaluates the underlying command instead of prompting you. To be prompted on every unsandboxed retry even in auto mode, add an <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions#match-by-input-parameter\">ask rule</a> for <code>Bash(dangerouslyDisableSandbox:true)</code>.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">You can disable this escape hatch by setting <code>\"allowUnsandboxedCommands\": false</code> in your <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\">sandbox settings</a>. When disabled, which the <code>/sandbox</code> Overrides tab shows as <strong>Strict sandbox mode</strong>, the <code>dangerouslyDisableSandbox</code> parameter is completely ignored and all commands must run sandboxed or be explicitly listed in <code>excludedCommands</code>.</span>\n<div role=\"note\" aria-label=\"Info\" class=\"callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-neutral-200 bg-neutral-50 dark:border-neutral-700 dark:bg-white/10\" data-callout-type=\"info\"><div class=\"mt-0.5 w-4\" data-component-part=\"callout-icon\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"flex-none size-5 text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-300\"><path d=\"M 7.2 0 C 3.2231 0 0 3.2231 0 7.2 C 0 11.1768 3.2231 14.4 7.2 14.4 C 11.1768 14.4 14.4 11.1768 14.4 7.2 C 14.4 3.2231 11.1768 0 7.2 0 Z M 7.2 13.05 C 3.9741 13.05 1.35 10.4257 1.35 7.2 C 1.35 3.9743 3.9741 1.35 7.2 1.35 C 10.426 1.35 13.05 3.9743 13.05 7.2 C 13.05 10.4257 10.426 13.05 7.2 13.05 Z M 8.325 9.45 H 7.875 V 6.975 C 7.875 6.6038 7.5741 6.3 7.2 6.3 H 6.3 C 5.9288 6.3 5.625 6.6038 5.625 6.975 C 5.625 7.3462 5.9288 7.65 6.3 7.65 H 6.525 V 9.45 H 6.075 C 5.7038 9.45 5.4 9.7538 5.4 10.125 C 5.4 10.4962 5.7038 10.8 6.075 10.8 H 8.325 C 8.6977 10.8 9 10.4977 9 10.125 C 9 9.7523 8.6991 9.45 8.325 9.45 Z M 7.2 5.4 C 7.697 5.4 8.1 4.997 8.1 4.5 C 8.1 4.003 7.697 3.6 7.2 3.6 C 6.703 3.6 6.3 4.0022 6.3 4.5 C 6.3 4.9978 6.7022 5.4 7.2 5.4 Z\" fill=\"currentColor\" stroke=\"none\"></path></svg></div><div class=\"text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&amp;_code]:text-current! [&amp;_kbd]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:border-current [&amp;_strong]:text-current! text-neutral-800 dark:text-neutral-300\" data-component-part=\"callout-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">Auto-allow mode works independently of your permission mode setting. Even if you’re not in “accept edits” mode, sandboxed Bash commands will run automatically when auto-allow is enabled. This means Bash commands that modify files within the sandbox boundaries will execute without prompting, even when file edit tools would normally require approval.</span></div></div>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"configure-sandboxing\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#configure-sandboxing\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Configure sandboxing</span></h2>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Customize sandbox behavior through your <code>settings.json</code> file. See <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\">Settings</a> for the complete configuration reference.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">By default, sandboxed commands can write only to the current working directory and the session temp directory. If subprocess commands like <code>kubectl</code>, <code>terraform</code>, or <code>npm</code> need to write outside those directories, use <code>sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite</code> to grant access to specific paths:</span>\n<div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent\" numberoflines=\"8\" language=\"json\"><div data-floating-buttons=\"true\" class=\"absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden\"><div class=\"z-10 select-none\" data-state=\"closed\"><button class=\"size-[26px] flex items-center justify-center rounded-md backdrop-blur group/code-snippet-feedback-button\" id=\"code-snippet-feedback-button\" aria-label=\"Report incorrect code\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-4 shrink-0 text-gray-400 group-hover/code-snippet-feedback-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/code-snippet-feedback-button:text-white/60\"><path d=\"M9 5.431V9.569\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9 11.417C8.449 11.417 8 11.866 8 12.417C8 12.968 8.449 13.417 9 13.417C9.551 13.417 10 12.968 10 12.417C10 11.866 9.551 11.417 9 11.417Z\" fill=\"currentColor\" stroke=\"none\"></path><path d=\"M10.968 2.25H7.03301C6.50301 2.25 5.99401 2.461 5.61901 2.836L2.83701 5.618C2.46201 5.993 2.25101 6.502 2.25101 7.032V10.967C2.25101 11.497 2.46201 12.006 2.83701 12.381L5.61901 15.163C5.99401 15.538 6.50301 15.749 7.03301 15.749H10.968C11.498 15.749 12.007 15.538 12.382 15.163L15.164 12.381C15.539 12.006 15.75 11.497 15.75 10.967V7.032C15.75 6.502 15.539 5.993 15.164 5.618L12.382 2.836C12.007 2.461 11.498 2.25 10.968 2.25Z\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></button></div><div class=\"z-10 select-none\" data-state=\"closed\"><button class=\"size-[26px] flex items-center justify-center rounded-md backdrop-blur group/copy-button\" data-testid=\"copy-code-button\" aria-label=\"Copy the contents from the code block\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" style=\"position:relative;--scroll-area-corner-height:0px;--scroll-area-corner-width:0px;font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E\" data-component-part=\"code-block-root\" class=\"w-0 min-w-full max-w-full h-full dark:bg-codeblock text-sm leading-6 transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background **:ring-0 **:outline-0 **:focus:ring-0 **:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white\"><div role=\"presentation\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_p7tbf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] py-3.5 px-4 overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"min-w-full! h-full children:my-0! children:shadow-none! children:bg-transparent!\"><div class=\"font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6\"><pre class=\"shiki shiki-themes github-light-default dark-plus\" style=\"background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E;color:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\" language=\"json\"><code language=\"json\" numberoflines=\"8\"><span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">{</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">  \"sandbox\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: {</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">    \"enabled\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6\">true</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">,</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">    \"filesystem\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: {</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">      \"allowWrite\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: [</span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"~/.kube\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">, </span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"/tmp/build\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">]</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">    }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">  }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">}</span></span>\n</code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">These paths are enforced at the OS level, so all commands running inside the sandbox, including their child processes, respect them. This is the recommended approach when a tool needs write access to a specific location, rather than excluding the tool from the sandbox entirely with <code>excludedCommands</code>.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">When the same filesystem array is defined in multiple <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#settings-precedence\">settings scopes</a>, the arrays are merged: paths from every scope are combined, not replaced.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Path prefixes control how paths are resolved:</span>\n<!-- --><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"position:relative;--scroll-area-corner-height:0px;--scroll-area-corner-width:0px\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area\" data-table-wrapper=\"true\" class=\"min-w-0 [--page-padding:20px] flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-(--page-padding) max-w-none [contain:inline-size]\"><div role=\"region\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_8tbf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\" aria-label=\"Scrollable table\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"flex\"><div class=\"px-(--page-padding) grow max-w-none table\"><table class=\"m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&amp;_td]:min-w-[150px] [&amp;_th]:text-left [&amp;_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums\"><thead><tr><th style=\"text-align:left\">Prefix</th><th style=\"text-align:left\">Meaning</th><th style=\"text-align:left\">Example</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td data-numeric=\"true\" style=\"text-align:left\"><code>/</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Absolute path from filesystem root</td><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>/tmp/build</code> stays <code>/tmp/build</code></td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>~/</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Relative to home directory</td><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>~/.kube</code> becomes <code>$HOME/.kube</code></td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>./</code> or no prefix</td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Relative to the project root for project settings, or to <code>~/.claude</code> for user settings</td><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>./output</code> in <code>.claude/settings.json</code> resolves to <code>&lt;project-root&gt;/output</code></td></tr></tbody></table></div></div></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">This syntax differs from <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions#read-and-edit\">Read and Edit permission rules</a>, which use <code>//path</code> for absolute and <code>/path</code> for project-relative. Sandbox filesystem paths use standard conventions: <code>/tmp/build</code> is absolute.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">You can also deny write or read access using <code>sandbox.filesystem.denyWrite</code> and <code>sandbox.filesystem.denyRead</code>, and re-allow specific paths within a denied region using <code>sandbox.filesystem.allowRead</code>. When read rules overlap, the more specific path wins:</span>\n<!-- --><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"position:relative;--scroll-area-corner-height:0px;--scroll-area-corner-width:0px\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area\" data-table-wrapper=\"true\" class=\"min-w-0 [--page-padding:20px] flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-(--page-padding) max-w-none [contain:inline-size]\"><div role=\"region\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_9lbf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\" aria-label=\"Scrollable table\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"flex\"><div class=\"px-(--page-padding) grow max-w-none table\"><table class=\"m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&amp;_td]:min-w-[150px] [&amp;_th]:text-left [&amp;_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums\"><thead><tr><th style=\"text-align:left\">Example rules</th><th style=\"text-align:left\">Result</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>\"denyRead\": [\"~/\"]</code> with <code>\"allowRead\": [\"~/projects\"]</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>~/projects</code> is readable and the rest of the home directory stays blocked. The narrower allow re-opens that part of the denied region</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>\"allowRead\": [\"~/\"]</code> with <code>\"denyRead\": [\"~/.env\"]</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>~/.env</code> stays blocked and the rest of the home directory is readable. An exact deny holds inside a wider allow, so a broad allow can’t silently re-expose a secret</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The example below blocks reading from the entire home directory while still allowing reads from the current project. Place it in your project’s <code>.claude/settings.json</code>, because the relative path <code>.</code> resolves to the project root only when the configuration lives in project settings:</span>\n<div whitespace-pre leading-6\"><pre class=\"shiki shiki-themes github-light-default dark-plus\" style=\"background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E;color:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\" language=\"json\"><code language=\"json\" numberoflines=\"9\"><span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">{</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">  \"sandbox\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: {</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">    \"enabled\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6\">true</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">,</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">    \"filesystem\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: {</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">      \"denyRead\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: [</span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"~/\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">],</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">      \"allowRead\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: [</span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\".\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">]</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">    }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">  }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">}</span></span>\n</code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The <code>.</code> in <code>allowRead</code> resolves to the project root because this configuration lives in project settings. If you placed the same configuration in <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code>, <code>.</code> would resolve to <code>~/.claude</code> instead, and project files would remain blocked by the <code>denyRead</code> rule.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"protect-credentials\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#protect-credentials\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Protect credentials</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The <code>sandbox.credentials</code> setting declares credential files and environment variables to protect from sandboxed commands. Each entry names a file path or an environment variable and a <code>mode</code>. The dedicated <code>credentials</code> block keeps credential rules grouped together and separate from general filesystem rules. Requires Claude Code v2.1.187 or later.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">For entries with <code>\"mode\": \"deny\"</code>, file paths are denied for reads inside the sandbox, the same restriction that <code>filesystem.denyRead</code> applies, and environment variables are unset before each sandboxed command runs.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The example below blocks reads of the AWS credentials file and the SSH directory and removes <code>GITHUB_TOKEN</code> and <code>NPM_TOKEN</code> from the environment of sandboxed commands:</span>\n<div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent\" numberoflines=\"15\" language=\"json\"><div data-floating-buttons=\"true\" class=\"absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden\"><div class=\"z-10 select-none\" data-state=\"closed\"><button class=\"size-[26px] flex items-center justify-center rounded-md backdrop-blur group/code-snippet-feedback-button\" id=\"code-snippet-feedback-button\" aria-label=\"Report incorrect code\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-4 shrink-0 text-gray-400 group-hover/code-snippet-feedback-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">}</span></span>\n</code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">File entries support only <code>\"mode\": \"deny\"</code>. Environment variable entries also accept <code>\"mode\": \"mask\"</code>, described below.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">File paths follow the same <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-path-prefixes\">prefix rules</a> as <code>sandbox.filesystem.*</code> settings, and <code>deny</code> entries from every <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#settings-precedence\">settings scope</a> are merged. A <code>deny</code> entry only ever narrows access, so any scope can add one, but no scope can remove one that another scope added.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">There is no built-in credential deny list, so only the files and variables you list are restricted. The setting affects sandboxed Bash commands only. To strip Anthropic and cloud provider credentials from all subprocesses regardless of sandboxing, set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/env-vars\"><code>CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB</code></a>.</span>\n<h4 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"mask-environment-variables\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#mask-environment-variables\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Mask environment variables</span></h4>\n<span data-as=\"p\"><code>\"mode\": \"mask\"</code> protects a credential while keeping the tools that authenticate with it working. <code>deny</code> removes the variable entirely, which also breaks tools that need it, such as <code>gh</code> or <code>npm</code>. Requires Claude Code v2.1.199 or later.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">With <code>mask</code>, the sandboxed command sees a per-session sentinel value instead of the real one. When a request leaves the sandbox for one of the credential’s <code>injectHosts</code>, the <a href=\"#network-isolation\" class=\"link\">sandbox proxy</a> replaces the sentinel with the real value. The command and anything it logs never hold the real credential, but its requests still authenticate.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The proxy substitutes the credential inside request contents, so it has to see them. Set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>network.tlsTerminate</code></a> so the proxy terminates TLS itself. Without it, masking fails closed: the command still sees only the sentinel, but the sentinel reaches the server unchanged and authentication fails. Claude Code reports this misconfiguration at startup.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The example below masks two tokens. <code>GH_TOKEN</code> is substituted only on requests to <code>api.github.com</code>, while <code>NPM_TOKEN</code> has no <code>injectHosts</code> and is substituted on requests to every host in <code>network.allowedDomains</code>. Each <code>injectHosts</code> entry must itself be covered by <code>network.allowedDomains</code>.</span>\n<div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent\" numberoflines=\"15\" language=\"json\"><div data-floating-buttons=\"true\" class=\"absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden\"><div class=\"z-10 select-none\" data-state=\"closed\"><button class=\"size-[26px] flex items-center justify-center rounded-md backdrop-blur group/code-snippet-feedback-button\" id=\"code-snippet-feedback-button\" aria-label=\"Report incorrect code\"><svg style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">\"name\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"GH_TOKEN\"</span><span
