style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"mask\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">, </span><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">\"injectHosts\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: [</span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"api.github.com\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">] },</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">        { </span><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">\"name\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"NPM_TOKEN\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">, </span><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">\"mode\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178\">\"mask\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\"> }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">      ]</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">    }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">  }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">}</span></span>\n</code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Unlike <code>deny</code>, masking authorizes the proxy to send your real credential to the listed hosts, so it is honored only from settings you or your administrator control: user settings, managed settings, and the <code>--settings</code> CLI flag. <code>mask</code> entries, <code>network.tlsTerminate</code>, and <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>credentials.allowPlaintextInject</code></a> in a repository’s <code>.claude/settings.json</code> or <code>.claude/settings.local.json</code> are ignored.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">When the same variable is listed with <code>deny</code> in any scope, <code>deny</code> takes precedence.</span>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"how-sandboxing-works\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#how-sandboxing-works\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">How sandboxing works</span></h2>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"filesystem-isolation\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#filesystem-isolation\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Filesystem isolation</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The sandboxed Bash tool restricts file system access to specific directories:</span>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Default write behavior</strong>: read and write access to the current working directory and its subdirectories, plus the session temp directory that <code>$TMPDIR</code> points to</li>\n<li><strong>Default read behavior</strong>: read access to the entire computer, except certain denied directories. Note that this default still allows reading credential files such as <code>~/.aws/credentials</code> and <code>~/.ssh/</code>. Use <a href=\"#protect-credentials\" class=\"link\"><code>sandbox.credentials</code></a> to block reads of these files and unset secret environment variables, or add the paths to <code>denyRead</code>.</li>\n<li><strong>Blocked access</strong>: cannot modify files outside the current working directory and session temp directory without explicit permission, including shell configuration files such as <code>~/.bashrc</code> and system binaries in <code>/bin/</code></li>\n<li><strong>Git worktrees</strong>: when the working directory is a <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/worktrees\">linked git worktree</a>, the sandbox also allows writes to the main repository’s shared <code>.git</code> directory so commands such as <code>git commit</code> can update refs and the index. Writes to <code>hooks/</code> and <code>config</code> inside that directory remain denied.</li>\n<li><strong>Configurable</strong>: define custom allowed and denied paths through settings</li>\n</ul>\n<span data-as=\"p\">You can grant write access to additional paths using <code>sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite</code> in your settings. These restrictions are enforced at the OS level, so they apply to all subprocess commands, including tools like <code>kubectl</code>, <code>terraform</code>, and <code>npm</code>, not just Claude’s file tools.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"network-isolation\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#network-isolation\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Network isolation</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Network access is controlled through a proxy server running outside the sandbox:</span>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Domain restrictions</strong>: no domains are pre-allowed by default. The first time a command needs a new domain, Claude Code prompts for approval. <!-- -->As of v2.1.191, choosing Yes allows the host for the rest of the current session, so later connections to the same host do not prompt again. Pre-allow domains with <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>allowedDomains</code></a> to avoid the prompt entirely. <code>WebFetch</code> allow rules also pre-allow domains, as described in <a href=\"#permission-rules\" class=\"link\">Permission rules</a>.</li>\n<li><strong>Managed lockdown</strong>: if <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>allowManagedDomainsOnly</code></a> is set in managed settings, non-allowed domains are blocked automatically instead of prompting, and only <code>allowedDomains</code> and <code>WebFetch(domain:...)</code> allow rules from managed settings are honored.</li>\n<li><strong>Custom proxy support</strong>: advanced users can implement custom rules on outgoing traffic</li>\n<li><strong>Comprehensive coverage</strong>: restrictions apply to all scripts, programs, and subprocesses spawned by commands</li>\n</ul>\n<div role=\"note\" aria-label=\"Note\" class=\"callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-600/20\" data-callout-type=\"note\"><div class=\"mt-0.5 w-4\" data-component-part=\"callout-icon\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-4 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300\"><path d=\"M 9 1.6714 C 13.0371 1.6714 16.3286 4.9629 16.3286 9 C 16.3286 13.0371 13.0371 16.3286 9 16.3286 C 7.0574 16.3252 5.1953 15.552 3.8217 14.1783 C 2.448 12.8047 1.6748 10.9426 1.6714 9 C 1.6714 4.9629 4.9629 1.6714 9 1.6714 Z M 9 0 C 4.0371 0 0 4.0371 0 9 C 0 13.9629 4.0371 18 9 18 C 13.9629 18 18 13.9629 18 9 C 18 4.0371 13.9629 0 9 0 Z M 10.2857 3.8571 H 7.7143 V 10.2857 H 10.2857 V 3.8571 Z M 10.2857 11.5714 H 7.7143 V 14.1429 H 10.2857 V 11.5714 Z\" fill=\"currentColor\" stroke=\"none\" fill-rule=\"evenodd\" clip-rule=\"evenodd\"></path></svg></div><div class=\"text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&amp;_code]:text-current! [&amp;_kbd]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:border-current [&amp;_strong]:text-current! text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300\" data-component-part=\"callout-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">The built-in proxy enforces the allowlist based on the requested hostname and, by default, does not terminate or inspect TLS traffic. <!-- -->The experimental <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>network.tlsTerminate</code></a> setting, available in Claude Code v2.1.199 and later, makes the built-in proxy terminate TLS itself, which <a href=\"#protect-credentials\" class=\"link\"><code>mask</code> credential entries</a> require. See <a href=\"#security-limitations\" class=\"link\">Security limitations</a> for the implications of the default, and <a href=\"#custom-proxy-configuration\" class=\"link\">Custom proxy configuration</a> if your threat model requires TLS inspection.</span></div></div>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"os-level-enforcement\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#os-level-enforcement\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">OS-level enforcement</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The sandboxed Bash tool leverages operating system security primitives:</span>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>macOS</strong>: uses Seatbelt for sandbox enforcement</li>\n<li><strong>Linux</strong>: uses <a href=\"https://github.com/containers/bubblewrap\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer\" class=\"link\">bubblewrap</a> for isolation</li>\n<li><strong>WSL2</strong>: uses bubblewrap, same as Linux</li>\n</ul>\n<span data-as=\"p\">WSL1 is not supported because bubblewrap requires kernel features only available in WSL2. These OS-level restrictions ensure that all child processes spawned by Claude Code’s commands inherit the same security boundaries.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">These same primitives are available as the standalone <a href=\"https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer\" class=\"link\"><code>@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime</code></a> package, which the <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/sandbox-environments#sandbox-runtime\">Sandbox environments</a> page covers as a separate approach for wrapping the entire Claude Code process.</span>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"how-sandboxing-relates-to-permissions-and-permission-modes\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#how-sandboxing-relates-to-permissions-and-permission-modes\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">How sandboxing relates to permissions and permission modes</span></h2>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Sandboxing, <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions\">permission rules</a>, and <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes\">permission modes</a> are complementary layers. The sections below cover how the sandbox interacts with each.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"permission-rules\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#permission-rules\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Permission rules</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Permission rules and sandboxing control different things:</span>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Permission rules</strong> control which tools Claude Code can use and are evaluated before any tool runs. They apply to all tools: Bash, Read, Edit, WebFetch, MCP, and others.</li>\n<li><strong>Sandboxing</strong> provides OS-level enforcement that restricts what Bash commands can access at the filesystem and network level. It applies only to Bash commands and their child processes.</li>\n</ul>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The two layers also differ in how they are enforced. Claude Code evaluates permission decisions before a command runs, based on the command string and, in auto mode, a separate classifier’s judgment about whether the command is safe. The operating system enforces the sandbox boundary on the running process, so it holds regardless of what the model chose to run and even if an allowed command does more than its name suggests.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Filesystem and network restrictions are configured through both sandbox settings and permission rules:</span>\n<!-- --><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"position:relative;--scroll-area-corner-height:0px;--scroll-area-corner-width:0px\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area\" data-table-wrapper=\"true\" class=\"min-w-0 [--page-padding:20px] flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-(--page-padding) max-w-none [contain:inline-size]\"><div role=\"region\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_jtbf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\" aria-label=\"Scrollable table\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"flex\"><div class=\"px-(--page-padding) grow max-w-none table\"><table class=\"m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&amp;_td]:min-w-[150px] [&amp;_th]:text-left [&amp;_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums\"><thead><tr><th style=\"text-align:left\">Setting or rule</th><th style=\"text-align:left\">What it does</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Grants subprocess write access to paths outside the working directory</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>sandbox.filesystem.denyWrite</code> and <code>sandbox.filesystem.denyRead</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Block subprocess access to specific paths</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>sandbox.filesystem.allowRead</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Re-allows reading specific paths within a <code>denyRead</code> region</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>Edit</code> allow rules</td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Grant write access to specific paths, the same way <code>sandbox.filesystem.allowWrite</code> does</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>Read</code> and <code>Edit</code> deny rules</td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Block access to specific files or directories</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>WebFetch</code> allow and deny rules</td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Control domain access</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\">Sandbox <code>allowedDomains</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Controls which domains Bash commands can reach</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\">Sandbox <code>deniedDomains</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Blocks specific domains even when a broader <code>allowedDomains</code> wildcard would otherwise permit them</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Paths from both <code>sandbox.filesystem</code> settings and permission rules are merged together into the final sandbox configuration.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The <a href=\"https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/examples/settings\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer\" class=\"link\">claude-code repository’s examples directory</a> includes starter settings configurations for common deployment scenarios, including sandbox-specific examples. Use these as starting points and adjust them to fit your needs.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"permission-modes\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#permission-modes\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Permission modes</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\"><code>/sandbox</code> is not a <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes\">permission mode</a>. Permission modes decide whether a tool call runs and whether you are prompted first, while the sandbox restricts what a Bash command can access once it runs. They differ in what they control and what replaces the per-action prompt:</span>\n<!-- --><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"position:relative;--scroll-area-corner-height:0px;--scroll-area-corner-width:0px\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area\" data-table-wrapper=\"true\" class=\"min-w-0 [--page-padding:20px] flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-(--page-padding) max-w-none [contain:inline-size]\"><div role=\"region\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_l5bf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\" aria-label=\"Scrollable table\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"flex\"><div class=\"px-(--page-padding) grow max-w-none table\"><table class=\"m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&amp;_td]:min-w-[150px] [&amp;_th]:text-left [&amp;_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums\"><thead><tr><th style=\"text-align:left\"></th><th style=\"text-align:left\">What it controls</th><th style=\"text-align:left\">What replaces the prompt</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>/sandbox</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">What a Bash command can access once it runs</td><td style=\"text-align:left\">The sandbox boundary itself, in <a href=\"#sandbox-modes\" class=\"link\">auto-allow mode</a></td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes#eliminate-prompts-with-auto-mode\">Auto mode</a></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Whether each tool call runs</td><td style=\"text-align:left\">A classifier that reviews actions</td></tr><tr><td style=\"text-align:left\"><code>--dangerously-skip-permissions</code></td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Whether each tool call runs</td><td style=\"text-align:left\">Nothing. <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes#protected-paths\">Protected path</a> checks are also skipped; only explicit <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions#manage-permissions\">ask rules</a>, connector tools <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/mcp#organization-controls-on-connector-tools\">your organization set to <code>ask</code></a>, MCP tools marked <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/mcp#require-approval-for-a-specific-tool\"><code>requiresUserInteraction</code></a>, and removing <code>/</code> or your home directory still prompt</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The sandbox’s <a href=\"#sandbox-modes\" class=\"link\">auto-allow mode</a> is separate from <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permission-modes#eliminate-prompts-with-auto-mode\">auto mode</a>: auto-allow approves Bash commands because the sandbox boundary contains them, while auto mode uses a classifier to review actions. The two work independently and can be combined. To choose an isolation boundary for unattended runs, see <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/sandbox-environments#how-isolation-relates-to-permission-modes\">Sandbox environments</a>.</span>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"configure-the-sandbox-for-your-organization\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#configure-the-sandbox-for-your-organization\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Configure the sandbox for your organization</span></h2>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Administrators can require sandboxing for every user, keep developers from widening the policy, and route sandbox traffic through a corporate proxy.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Enforce sandboxing with managed settings</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">To require the sandbox for every developer, deliver the <code>sandbox</code> keys through <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#settings-files\">managed settings</a>, either as a file managed by your MDM or sandbox, refuses to start Claude Code if the sandbox cannot initialize, and prevents the model from retrying commands outside the sandbox:</span>\n<div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose data-component-part=\"code-block-root\" class=\"w-0 min-w-full max-w-full h-full dark:bg-codeblock text-sm leading-6 transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background **:ring-0 **:outline-0 **:focus:ring-0 **:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white\"><div role=\"presentation\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_pmtbf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] py-3.5 px-4 overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"min-w-full! h-full children:my-0! children:shadow-none! children:bg-transparent!\"><div class=\"font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6\"><pre class=\"shiki shiki-themes github-light-default dark-plus\" style=\"background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E;color:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\" language=\"json\"><code language=\"json\" numberoflines=\"7\"><span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">{</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">  \"sandbox\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: {</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">    \"enabled\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6\">true</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">,</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">    \"failIfUnavailable\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6\">true</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">,</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">    \"allowUnsandboxedCommands\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: </span><span style=\"color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6\">false</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">  }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">}</span></span>\n</code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The two keys beyond <code>enabled</code> control what happens when the sandbox cannot run a command:</span>\n<ul>\n<li><strong><code>failIfUnavailable</code></strong>: a missing dependency such as bubblewrap on Linux blocks Claude Code from starting rather than showing a warning and falling back to unsandboxed execution</li>\n<li><strong><code>allowUnsandboxedCommands: false</code></strong>: the <code>dangerouslyDisableSandbox</code> escape hatch is ignored, so commands that fail under the sandbox cannot be retried outside it</li>\n</ul>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Two additions are worth considering alongside them. Add <code>excludedCommands</code> for any organization-approved tools that must run without isolation. Add <a href=\"#protect-credentials\" class=\"link\"><code>sandbox.credentials</code></a> entries for credential directories such as <code>~/.aws</code> and <code>~/.ssh</code> and for secret environment variables, since the default read policy still allows them.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The sandbox does not run on native Windows, so if your fleet includes Windows hosts, scope this configuration to macOS and Linux or have those users run Claude Code inside WSL2 or a container.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"keep-developers-from-widening-the-policy\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#keep-developers-from-widening-the-policy\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Keep developers from widening the policy</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">For boolean keys such as <code>enabled</code> and <code>failIfUnavailable</code>, Claude Code uses the managed value and ignores anything a developer sets locally. For array keys such as <code>excludedCommands</code> and <code>allowRead</code>, Claude Code merges entries from every scope, so a developer can append entries that widen the policy.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Set <code>allowManagedReadPathsOnly</code> to <code>true</code> in managed settings so that only <code>allowRead</code> entries from managed settings are honored. User, project, and local <code>allowRead</code> entries are ignored. This prevents developers from widening read access beyond the organization-approved paths. To lock network domains to the managed values the same way, set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>allowManagedDomainsOnly</code></a>.</span>\n<span data-as=\"p\"><code>excludedCommands</code> has no equivalent managed-only lockdown, so a developer can always append entries that run additional commands outside the sandbox. Keep the managed list narrow.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"custom-proxy-configuration\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#custom-proxy-configuration\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Custom proxy configuration</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">For organizations requiring advanced network security, you can implement a custom proxy to:</span>\n<ul>\n<li>Decrypt and inspect HTTPS traffic</li>\n<li>Apply custom filtering rules</li>\n<li>Log all network requests</li>\n<li>Integrate with existing security infrastructure</li>\n</ul>\n<span data-as=\"p\">To point Claude Code at your proxy, set the proxy ports in <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\">sandbox settings</a>:</span>\n<div class=\"code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent\" numberoflines=\"8\" language=\"json\"><div data-floating-buttons=\"true\" class=\"absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden\"><div class=\"z-10 select-none\" data-state=\"closed\"><button class=\"size-[26px] flex items-center justify-center rounded-md backdrop-blur group/code-snippet-feedback-button\" group-hover/code-snippet-feedback-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/code-snippet-feedback-button:text-white/60\"><path d=\"M9 5.431V9.569\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" style=\"position:relative;--scroll-area-corner-height:0px;--scroll-area-corner-width:0px;font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E\" data-component-part=\"code-block-root\" class=\"w-0 min-w-full max-w-full h-full dark:bg-codeblock text-sm leading-6 transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background **:ring-0 **:outline-0 **:focus:ring-0 **:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white\"><div role=\"presentation\" data-id=\"base-ui-_R_pq5bf7av5tccsmisnpfiulb_-viewport\" tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"size-full rounded-[inherit] py-3.5 px-4 overflow-y-hidden! base-ui-disable-scrollbar\" style=\"overflow:scroll\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-viewport\"><div role=\"presentation\" style=\"min-width:fit-content\" data-component-part=\"scroll-area-content\" class=\"min-w-full! h-full children:my-0! children:shadow-none! children:bg-transparent!\"><div class=\"font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6\"><pre class=\"shiki shiki-themes github-light-default dark-plus\" style=\"background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E;color:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\" language=\"json\"><code language=\"json\" numberoflines=\"8\"><span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">{</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\">  \"sandbox\"</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">: {</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#B5CEA8\">8080</span><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">,</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE\"> style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">    }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">  }</span></span>\n<span class=\"line\"><span style=\"color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4\">}</span></span>\n</code></pre></div></div></div></div><div class=\"print:hidden\" data-fade-overlay=\"true\" aria-hidden=\"true\" style=\"--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E\"></div></div>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"troubleshooting\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#troubleshooting\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Troubleshooting</span></h2>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Some commands fail inside the sandbox even though they work outside it. The fixes below cover the most common cases.</span>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Commands fail with a host-not-allowed error</strong>: many CLI tools need to reach specific hosts. Granting permission when prompted adds the host to your allowed list so the tool runs inside the sandbox in future.</li>\n<li><strong><code>jest</code> hangs or fails</strong>: <code>watchman</code> is incompatible with the sandbox. Run <code>jest --no-watchman</code> instead.</li>\n<li><strong>Go-based CLIs fail TLS verification on macOS</strong>: tools such as <code>gh</code>, <code>gcloud</code>, and <code>terraform</code> may fail TLS verification under Seatbelt. List these tools in <code>excludedCommands</code> to run them outside the sandbox. If you are using <code>httpProxyPort</code> with a MITM proxy and custom CA, set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>enableWeakerNetworkIsolation</code></a> to <code>true</code> instead.</li>\n<li><strong><code>open</code>, <code>osascript</code>, or browser-based auth flows fail with error <code>-600</code> on macOS</strong>: the sandbox blocks Apple Events by default. Set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>allowAppleEvents</code></a> to <code>true</code> in your user, managed, or CLI settings to allow them. Project settings are ignored for this key. Enabling it removes code-execution isolation, since sandboxed commands can then launch other applications unsandboxed with no user prompt and send AppleScript commands to running applications, subject to the macOS automation-consent prompt (TCC). Alternatively, add the command to <code>excludedCommands</code> to run it outside the sandbox.</li>\n<li><strong><code>docker</code> commands fail</strong>: <code>docker</code> is incompatible with the sandbox. Add <code>docker *</code> to <code>excludedCommands</code> to run it outside the sandbox.</li>\n<li><strong>Bubblewrap fails to start inside a container</strong>: in an unprivileged container, bubblewrap cannot mount a fresh <code>/proc</code> filesystem. Set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>enableWeakerNestedSandbox</code></a> to <code>true</code> so the inner sandbox bind-mounts the container’s existing <code>/proc</code> instead. Only use this setting when the outer container already provides the isolation boundary you need, since it exposes process information to sandboxed commands that a fresh <code>/proc</code> mount would hide.</li>\n<li><strong>Seccomp filter on Linux</strong>: the seccomp filter is required to block Unix domain sockets. The Dependencies tab in <code>/sandbox</code> appears only when a dependency is missing; if you don’t see the tab after a restart, the filter is already installed. If the filter is missing, run <code>npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime</code> to install the helper, then restart Claude Code so the startup dependency check detects it.</li>\n<li><strong><code>--dangerously-skip-permissions</code> fails as root</strong>: this flag is blocked when running as root or via sudo on Linux and macOS, because root access combined with no permission prompts can modify any file or service on the system. The check is skipped automatically inside a recognized sandbox. To run autonomously in a container, use the <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/devcontainer\">dev container</a> configuration, which runs Claude Code as a non-root user.</li>\n</ul>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"limitations\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#limitations\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Limitations</span></h2>\n<span data-as=\"p\">Sandboxing reduces risk but is not a complete isolation boundary. Review the limitations below before relying on it as a hard security control.</span>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"security-limitations\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#security-limitations\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Security limitations</span></h3>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Network filtering</strong>: the sandbox restricts which domains processes can connect to. By default the built-in proxy does not terminate or inspect TLS on outbound traffic, so the contents of encrypted connections are not examined. The experimental <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/settings#sandbox-settings\"><code>network.tlsTerminate</code></a> setting terminates TLS at the proxy for <a href=\"#protect-credentials\" class=\"link\"><code>mask</code> credential substitution</a> but does not add content filtering. You are responsible for ensuring that only trusted domains are allowed in your policy.</li>\n</ul>\n<div role=\"note\" aria-label=\"Warning\" class=\"callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-yellow-200 bg-yellow-50 dark:border-yellow-900 dark:bg-yellow-600/20 [&amp;_[data-component-part='callout-icon']]:mt-px\" data-callout-type=\"warning\"><div class=\"mt-0.5 w-4\" data-component-part=\"callout-icon\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"flex-none size-5 text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-300\"><path d=\"M 9 6.75 v 1.5 m 0 3 h 0.0075 m -5.2035 3 h 10.392 c 1.155 0 1.8765 -1.2503 1.299 -2.25 L 10.299 3 c -0.5775 -0.9997 -2.0205 -0.9997 -2.598 0 L 2.505 12 c -0.5775 0.9997 0.144 2.25 1.299 2.25 z\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div><div class=\"text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&amp;_code]:text-current! [&amp;_kbd]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:border-current [&amp;_strong]:text-current! text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-300\" data-component-part=\"callout-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">Allowing broad domains such as <code>github.com</code> can create paths for data exfiltration. Because the proxy makes its allow decision from the client-supplied hostname without inspecting TLS, code running inside the sandbox can potentially use <a href=\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domain_fronting\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer\" class=\"link\">domain fronting</a> or similar techniques to reach hosts outside the allowlist. If your threat model requires stronger guarantees, configure a <a href=\"#custom-proxy-configuration\" class=\"link\">custom proxy</a> that terminates TLS and inspects traffic, and install its CA certificate inside the sandbox. Stronger TLS-aware network isolation is an active area of development.</span></div></div>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Privilege escalation via Unix sockets</strong>: the <code>allowUnixSockets</code> configuration can inadvertently grant access to powerful system services that could lead to sandbox bypasses. For example, allowing access to <code>/var/run/docker.sock</code> effectively grants access to the host system through the Docker socket. Consider carefully any Unix sockets that you allow through the sandbox.</li>\n<li><strong>Filesystem permission escalation</strong>: overly broad filesystem write permissions can enable privilege escalation attacks. Allowing writes to directories containing executables in <code>$PATH</code>, system configuration directories, or user shell configuration files such as <code>.bashrc</code> or <code>.zshrc</code> can lead to code execution in different security contexts when other users or system processes access these files.</li>\n<li><strong>Linux sandbox strength</strong>: the Linux implementation provides strong filesystem and network isolation but includes an <code>enableWeakerNestedSandbox</code> mode that enables it to work inside Docker environments without privileged namespaces, or on Linux hosts where unprivileged user namespaces are disabled by sysctl. This option considerably weakens security and should only be used when additional isolation is otherwise enforced.</li>\n<li><strong>Apple Events on macOS</strong>: the macOS sandbox blocks Apple Events by default. The <code>allowAppleEvents</code> setting lifts this restriction so tools such as <code>open</code> and <code>osascript</code> work, but it removes code-execution isolation: sandboxed commands can launch other applications unsandboxed with no user prompt, and can send AppleScript commands to running applications, subject to the per-app macOS automation-consent prompt (TCC). It is only honored from user, managed, or CLI settings. Project settings cannot enable it.</li>\n<li><strong>Settings files protected</strong>: the sandbox automatically denies write access to Claude Code’s <code>settings.json</code> files at every scope and to the managed settings directory, so a sandboxed command cannot modify its own policy. <!-- -->In v2.1.210 and later, the deny rules resolve symlinks: when a symlink appears at a protected settings file path after startup, the sandbox adds its target to the deny list for the next command, so a linked settings file can’t be edited through the link.</li>\n</ul>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"platform-and-tool-compatibility\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#platform-and-tool-compatibility\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Platform and tool compatibility</span></h3>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Platform support</strong>: supports macOS, Linux, and WSL2. WSL1 and native Windows are not supported.</li>\n<li><strong>Performance overhead</strong>: minimal, but some filesystem operations may be slightly slower.</li>\n<li><strong>Tool compatibility</strong>: some tools that require specific system access patterns may need configuration adjustments, or may need to be run outside the sandbox.</li>\n</ul>\n<h3 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"scope\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#scope\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">Scope</span></h3>\n<span data-as=\"p\">The sandbox isolates Bash subprocesses. Other tools operate under different boundaries:</span>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Built-in file tools</strong>: Read, Edit, and Write use the permission system directly rather than running through the sandbox. See <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/permissions\">permissions</a>.</li>\n<li><strong>Computer use</strong>: when Claude opens apps and controls your screen, it runs on your actual desktop rather than in an isolated environment. Per-app permission prompts gate each application. See <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/computer-use\">computer use in the CLI</a> or <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/desktop#let-claude-use-your-computer\">computer use in Desktop</a>.</li>\n<li><strong>Environment variables</strong>: sandboxed Bash commands inherit the parent process environment by default, including any credentials set there. Use <a href=\"#protect-credentials\" class=\"link\"><code>sandbox.credentials</code></a> to unset or mask specific variables for sandboxed commands, or set <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/env-vars\"><code>CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB</code></a> to strip Anthropic and cloud provider credentials from all subprocesses.</li>\n<li><strong>Subagents</strong>: <a class=\"link\" href=\"/docs/en/sub-agents\">subagents</a> run in the same process as the parent session and use the same sandbox configuration. Bash commands inside a subagent are sandboxed when sandboxing is enabled in the parent session.</li>\n</ul>\n<div role=\"note\" aria-label=\"Warning\" class=\"callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-yellow-200 bg-yellow-50 dark:border-yellow-900 dark:bg-yellow-600/20 [&amp;_[data-component-part='callout-icon']]:mt-px\" data-callout-type=\"warning\"><div class=\"mt-0.5 w-4\" data-component-part=\"callout-icon\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"flex-none size-5 text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-300\"><path d=\"M 9 6.75 v 1.5 m 0 3 h 0.0075 m -5.2035 3 h 10.392 c 1.155 0 1.8765 -1.2503 1.299 -2.25 L 10.299 3 c -0.5775 -0.9997 -2.0205 -0.9997 -2.598 0 L 2.505 12 c -0.5775 0.9997 0.144 2.25 1.299 2.25 z\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"1.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div><div class=\"text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&amp;_code]:text-current! [&amp;_kbd]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:text-current! [&amp;_a]:border-current [&amp;_strong]:text-current! text-yellow-800 dark:text-yellow-300\" data-component-part=\"callout-content\"><span data-as=\"p\">Effective sandboxing requires both filesystem and network isolation. Without network isolation, a compromised agent could exfiltrate sensitive files like SSH keys. Without filesystem isolation, a compromised agent could backdoor system resources to gain network access. When you widen the defaults, check that an <code>allowWrite</code> path, a broad <code>allowedDomains</code> entry, or an <code>excludedCommands</code> exception does not undo a restriction on the other side.</span></div></div>\n<h2 class=\"flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold\" id=\"see-also\"><div class=\"absolute\" tabindex=\"-1\"><a href=\"#see-also\" class=\"-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link\" aria-label=\"Navigate to header\">​<div class=\"size-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light\"><svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 18 18\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" aria-hidden=\"true\" class=\"size-3 shrink-0\"><path d=\"M8.50001 6.827C8.14801 6.995 7.81801 7.225 7.52701 7.517L7.51701 7.527C6.13601 8.908 6.13601 11.146 7.51701 12.527L9.69201 14.702C11.073 16.083 13.311 16.083 14.692 14.702L14.702 14.692C16.083 13.311 16.083 11.073 14.702 9.692L13.771 8.761\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path><path d=\"M9.50002 11.173C9.85202 11.005 10.182 10.775 10.473 10.483L10.483 10.473C11.864 9.092 11.864 6.854 10.483 5.473L8.30802 3.298C6.92702 1.917 4.68902 1.917 3.30802 3.298L3.29802 3.308C1.91702 4.689 1.91702 6.927 3.29802 8.308L4.22902 9.239\" stroke=\"currentColor\" width=\"1.5\" linecap=\"round\" linejoin=\"round\"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class=\"cursor-pointer\">See also</span></h2>\n<ul>\n<li><a class=\"link\" * First-party analytics for code.claude.com/docs.\\n *\\n * Loads the SDK bundle (window.antalytics) from the CDN and initialises it.\\n * The bundle is rebuilt and republished automatically whenever the SDK\\n * source changes upstream, so this file rarely needs to change.\\n *\\n * Consent: events are gated on the anthropic-consent-preferences cookie\\n * written by the shared banner (consent-loader.js). The\\n * choice persists per browser via localStorage.\\n *\\n * The button is appended to document.body, outside the Next.js app root,\\n * so it survives client-side navigation. The locale directory comes\\n    // before any page slug, so the first matching segment wins.\\n    var segments = window.location.pathname.split('/')\\n    for (var i = 1; i \\u003c segments.length; label\\n  }\\n\\n  function start() {\\n    if (document.getElementById('toc-collapse-toggle')) return\\n\\n    var btn = document.createElement('button')\\n    btn.id = 'toc-collapse-toggle'\\n The Dependencies tab in \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"/sandbox\\\"\\n        }), \\\" appears only when a dependency is missing; if you don’t see the tab after a restart, the filter is already installed. If the filter is missing, run \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime\\\"\\n        }), \\\" to install the helper, then restart Claude Code so the startup dependency check detects it.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsxs(_components.strong, {\\n          children: [_jsx(_components.code, {\\n            children: \\\"--dangerously-skip-permissions\\\"\\n          }), \\\" fails as root\\\"]\\n        }), \\\": this flag is blocked when running as root or via sudo on Linux and macOS, because root access combined with no permission prompts can modify any file or service on the system. The check is skipped automatically inside a recognized sandbox. To run autonomously in a container, use the \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"/en/devcontainer\\\",\\n          children: \\\"dev container\\\"\\n        }), \\\" configuration, which runs Claude Code as a non-root user.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"2\\\",\\n      id: \\\"limitations\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Limitations\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(_components.p, {\\n      children: \\\"Sandboxing reduces risk but is not a complete isolation boundary. Review the limitations below before relying on it as a hard security control.\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"3\\\",\\n      id: \\\"security-limitations\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Security limitations\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.ul, {\\n      children: [\\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Network filtering\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the sandbox restricts which domains processes can connect to. By default the built-in proxy does not terminate or inspect TLS on outbound traffic, so the contents of encrypted connections are not examined. The experimental \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"/en/settings#sandbox-settings\\\",\\n          children: _jsx(_components.code, {\\n            children: \\\"network.tlsTerminate\\\"\\n          })\\n        }), \\\" setting terminates TLS at the proxy for \\\", _jsxs(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"#protect-credentials\\\",\\n          children: [_jsx(_components.code, {\\n            children: \\\"mask\\\"\\n          }), \\\" credential substitution\\\"]\\n        }), \\\" but does not add content filtering. You are responsible for ensuring that only trusted domains are allowed in your policy.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Warning, {\\n      children: _jsxs(_components.p, {\\n        children: [\\\"Allowing broad domains such as \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"github.com\\\"\\n        }), \\\" can create paths for data exfiltration. Because the proxy makes its allow decision from the client-supplied hostname without inspecting TLS, code running inside the sandbox can potentially use \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domain_fronting\\\",\\n          children: \\\"domain fronting\\\"\\n        }), \\\" or similar techniques to reach hosts outside the allowlist. If your threat model requires stronger guarantees, configure a \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"#custom-proxy-configuration\\\",\\n          children: \\\"custom proxy\\\"\\n        }), \\\" that terminates TLS and inspects traffic, and install its CA certificate inside the sandbox. Stronger TLS-aware network isolation is an active area of development.\\\"]\\n      })\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.ul, {\\n      children: [\\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Privilege escalation via Unix sockets\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"allowUnixSockets\\\"\\n        }), \\\" configuration can inadvertently grant access to powerful system services that could lead to sandbox bypasses. For example, allowing access to \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"/var/run/docker.sock\\\"\\n        }), \\\" effectively grants access to the host system through the Docker socket. Consider carefully any Unix sockets that you allow through the sandbox.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Filesystem permission escalation\\\"\\n        }), \\\": overly broad filesystem write permissions can enable privilege escalation attacks. Allowing writes to directories containing executables in \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"$PATH\\\"\\n        }), \\\", system configuration directories, or user shell configuration files such as \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\".bashrc\\\"\\n        }), \\\" or \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\".zshrc\\\"\\n        }), \\\" can lead to code execution in different security contexts when other users or system processes access these files.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Linux sandbox strength\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the Linux implementation provides strong filesystem and network isolation but includes an \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"enableWeakerNestedSandbox\\\"\\n        }), \\\" mode that enables it to work inside Docker environments without privileged namespaces, or on Linux hosts where unprivileged user namespaces are disabled by sysctl. This option considerably weakens security and should only be used when additional isolation is otherwise enforced.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Apple Events on macOS\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the macOS sandbox blocks Apple Events by default. The \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"allowAppleEvents\\\"\\n        }), \\\" setting lifts this restriction so tools such as \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"open\\\"\\n        }), \\\" and \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"osascript\\\"\\n        }), \\\" work, but it removes code-execution isolation: sandboxed commands can launch other applications unsandboxed with no user prompt, and can send AppleScript commands to running applications, subject to the per-app macOS automation-consent prompt (TCC). It is only honored from user, managed, or CLI settings. Project settings cannot enable it.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Settings files protected\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the sandbox automatically denies write access to Claude Code’s \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"settings.json\\\"\\n        }), \\\" files at every scope and to the managed settings directory, so a sandboxed command cannot modify its own policy. \\\", \\\"In v2.1.210 and later, the deny rules resolve symlinks: when a symlink appears at a protected settings file path after startup, the sandbox adds its target to the deny list for the next command, so a linked settings file can’t be edited through the link.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"3\\\",\\n      id: \\\"platform-and-tool-compatibility\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Platform and tool compatibility\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.ul, {\\n      children: [\\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Platform support\\\"\\n        }), \\\": supports macOS, Linux, and WSL2. WSL1 and native Windows are not supported.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Performance overhead\\\"\\n        }), \\\": minimal, but some filesystem operations may be slightly slower.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Tool compatibility\\\"\\n        }), \\\": some tools that require specific system access patterns may need configuration adjustments, or may need to be run outside the sandbox.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"3\\\",\\n      id: \\\"scope\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Scope\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(_components.p, {\\n      children: \\\"The sandbox isolates Bash subprocesses. Other tools operate under different boundaries:\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.ul, {\\n      children: [\\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Built-in file tools\\\"\\n        }), \\\": Read, Edit, and Write use the permission system directly rather than running through the sandbox. To strip Anthropic and cloud provider credentials from all subprocesses regardless of sandboxing, set \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n        href: \\\"/en/env-vars\\\",\\n        children: _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"CLAUDE_CODE_SUBPROCESS_ENV_SCRUB\\\"\\n        })\\n      }), \\\".\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"4\\\",\\n      id: \\\"mask-environment-variables\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Mask environment variables\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.p, {\\n      children: [_jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"\\\\\\\"mode\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"mask\\\\\\\"\\\"\\n      }), \\\" protects a credential while keeping the tools that authenticate with it working. \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"deny\\\"\\n      }), \\\" removes the variable entirely, which also breaks tools that need it, such as \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"gh\\\"\\n      }), \\\" or \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"npm\\\"\\n      }), \\\". Requires Claude Code v2.1.199 or later.\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.p, {\\n      children: [\\\"With \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"mask\\\"\\n      }), \\\", the sandboxed command sees a per-session sentinel value instead of the real one. When a request leaves the sandbox for one of the credential’s \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"injectHosts\\\"\\n      }), \\\", the \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n        href: \\\"#network-isolation\\\",\\n        children: \\\"sandbox proxy\\\"\\n      }), \\\" replaces the sentinel with the real value. The command and anything it logs never hold the real credential, but its requests still authenticate.\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.p, {\\n      children: [\\\"The proxy substitutes the credential inside request contents, so it has to see them. Set \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n        href: \\\"/en/settings#sandbox-settings\\\",\\n        children: _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"network.tlsTerminate\\\"\\n        })\\n      }), \\\" so the proxy terminates TLS itself. Without it, masking fails closed: the command still sees only the sentinel, but the sentinel reaches the server unchanged and authentication fails. Claude Code reports this misconfiguration at startup.\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.p, {\\n      children: [\\\"The example below masks two tokens. \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"GH_TOKEN\\\"\\n      }), \\\" is substituted only on requests to \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"api.github.com\\\"\\n      }), \\\", while \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"NPM_TOKEN\\\"\\n      }), \\\" has no \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"injectHosts\\\"\\n      }), \\\" and is substituted on requests to every host in \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n        children: \\\"network.allowedDomains\\\"\\n      }), \\\". proxy enforces the allowlist based on the requested hostname and, by default, does not terminate or inspect TLS traffic. \\\", \\\"The experimental \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"/en/settings#sandbox-settings\\\",\\n          children: _jsx(_components.code, {\\n            children: \\\"network.tlsTerminate\\\"\\n          })\\n        }), \\\" setting, available in Set \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"/en/settings#sandbox-settings\\\",\\n          children: _jsx(_components.code, {\\n            children: \\\"enableWeakerNestedSandbox\\\"\\n          })\\n        }), \\\" to \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"true\\\"\\n        }), \\\" so the inner sandbox bind-mounts the container’s existing \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"/proc\\\"\\n        }), \\\" instead. The Dependencies tab in \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"/sandbox\\\"\\n        }), \\\" appears only when a dependency is missing; if you don’t see the tab after a restart, the filter is already installed.
