The check is skipped automatically inside a recognized sandbox. To run autonomously in a container, use the \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"/en/devcontainer\\\",\\n          children: \\\"dev container\\\"\\n        }), \\\" configuration, which runs Claude Code as a non-root user.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"2\\\",\\n      id: \\\"limitations\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Limitations\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(_components.p, {\\n      children: \\\"Sandboxing reduces risk but is not a complete isolation boundary. Review the limitations below before relying on it as a hard security control.\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"3\\\",\\n      id: \\\"security-limitations\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Security limitations\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.ul, {\\n      children: [\\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Network filtering\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the sandbox restricts which domains processes can connect to. By default the built-in proxy does not terminate or inspect TLS on outbound traffic, so the contents of encrypted connections are not examined. The experimental \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"/en/settings#sandbox-settings\\\",\\n          children: _jsx(_components.code, {\\n            children: \\\"network.tlsTerminate\\\"\\n          })\\n        }), \\\" setting terminates TLS at the proxy for \\\", _jsxs(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"#protect-credentials\\\",\\n          children: [_jsx(_components.code, {\\n            children: \\\"mask\\\"\\n          }), \\\" credential substitution\\\"]\\n        }), \\\" but does not add content filtering. Because the proxy makes its allow decision from the client-supplied hostname without inspecting TLS, code running inside the sandbox can potentially use \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domain_fronting\\\",\\n          children: \\\"domain fronting\\\"\\n        }), \\\" or similar techniques to reach hosts outside the allowlist. If your threat model requires stronger guarantees, configure a \\\", _jsx(_components.a, {\\n          href: \\\"#custom-proxy-configuration\\\",\\n          children: \\\"custom proxy\\\"\\n        }), \\\" that terminates TLS and inspects traffic, and install its CA certificate inside the sandbox. Stronger TLS-aware network isolation is an active area of development.\\\"]\\n      })\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.ul, {\\n      children: [\\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Privilege escalation via Unix sockets\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"allowUnixSockets\\\"\\n        }), \\\" configuration can inadvertently grant access to powerful system services that could lead to sandbox bypasses. For example, allowing access to \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"/var/run/docker.sock\\\"\\n        }), \\\" effectively grants access to the host system through the Docker socket. It is only honored from user, managed, or CLI settings. Project settings cannot enable it.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Settings files protected\\\"\\n        }), \\\": the sandbox automatically denies write access to Claude Code’s \\\", _jsx(_components.code, {\\n          children: \\\"settings.json\\\"\\n        }), \\\" files at every scope and to the managed settings directory, so a sandboxed command cannot modify its own policy. \\\", \\\"In v2.1.210 and later, the deny rules resolve symlinks: when a symlink appears at a protected settings file path after startup, the sandbox adds its target to the deny list for the next command, so a linked settings file can’t be edited through the link.\\\"]\\n      }), \\\"\\\\n\\\"]\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsx(Heading, {\\n      level: \\\"3\\\",\\n      id: \\\"platform-and-tool-compatibility\\\",\\n      children: \\\"Platform and tool compatibility\\\"\\n    }), \\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.ul, {\\n      children: [\\\"\\\\n\\\", _jsxs(_components.li, {\\n        children: [_jsx(_components.strong, {\\n          children: \\\"Platform support\\\"\\n        }), \\\": supports macOS, Linux, and WSL2.
